LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE CHARTER OAK FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, AND, TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE DAMAGE TO THE INSURED. SEE GULF INS. CO. VS. TIG INSURANCE CO. (2001) 86 CAL.APP. 4TH, 422. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Hearing On Defts' Demurrer To The Second Cause Of Action In Pltf'S Complaint; Appendix Of Non-California State Court Authority Regarding Memorandum Of P/A In Support Of Defts' General Demurrer To Pltf'S Second Cause Of Action;
Off Calendar May-19-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE CHARTER OAK FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, and, TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA'S Demurrer To The Second Cause Of Action In Plaintiff's Complaint. Appearances: Joseph M. Nykodyn, Esq. Law Offices of Ryan & Lifter, (925) 884-2080, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Ramiro Morales, Esq. of Morales Fierro & Reeves, (925) 288-1776, appearing for Defendants and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege damage to the insured. See Gulf Ins. Co. vs. TIG Insurance Co. (2001) 86 Cal.App. 4th, 422.
(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
